page: Microsoft Azure D8as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The virtualization layer is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

This server configuration does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning workloads requiring high-speed local scratch disks or hardware-accelerated graphics processing must look elsewhere. The instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to provision external persistent storage volumes to meet their disk input and output requirements. Additionally, the configuration does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D8as_v7 offers a balanced allocation of compute and memory resources. This 1:4 ratio makes it a standard option for workloads that do not exhibit extreme demands for either memory capacity or raw compute power. Appropriate workloads for this profile include mid-tier web serving, application servers, development environments, and small to medium database hosting when paired with external Premium Storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the Dasv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. This instance does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for attaching external persistent volumes. It also lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Qualitatively, the D8as_v7 provides a standard resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads. It is designed for applications requiring a steady balance of compute and memory without the need for local disk performance or hardware acceleration. Typical use cases include web servers, application hosting, and development environments.

tagline: An x86_64 virtual machine offering eight dedicated AMD vCPUs and thirty-two gigabytes of RAM for balanced general-purpose workloads.

32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Premium Storage capable with no local storage included
